zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Cathcart於1395年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾卡夫卡特時，她的父親Alan Cathcart33歲，母親Janet Maxwell18歲。她於1415年與Sir John Wallace 10th of Riccarton在蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1424年8月17日在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安姆瑟爾包享年29歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安格雷布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Cathcart于1395年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾卡夫卡特时，她的父亲Alan Cathcart33岁，母亲Janet Maxwell18岁。她于1415年与Sir John Wallace 10th of Riccarton在苏格兰艾斯爾艾爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1424年8月17日在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 洛迪安姆瑟爾包享年29岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰米德洛迪安格雷布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Cathcart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Cathcart was born in 1395, in Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Scotland, her father, Alan Cathcart, was 33 and her mother, Janet Maxwell, was 18. She married Sir John Wallace 10th of Riccarton in 1415, in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 17 August 1424, in Musselburgh, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 29, and was buried in Gorebridge, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Cathcart est née en 1395, à Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, Alan Cathcart, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Janet Maxwell, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Wallace 10th of Riccarton en 1415, à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 17 août 1424, à Musselburgh, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterrée à Gorebridge, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Cathcart 1395 in Cathcart, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alan Cathcart 33 und ihre Mutter Janet Maxwell 18. Sie hat 1415 in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland Sir John Wallace 10th of Riccarton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 17. August 1424 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Musselburgh, East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Gorebridge, Midlothian, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

Morì il 17 agosto 1424, nel Musselburgh, East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 29 anni, e fu sepolta a Gorebridge, Midlothian, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Cathcartが1395年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアカスカートで生まれた時，父Alan Cathcartは33歳，母Janet Maxwellは18歳であった。1415年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアエイルでSir John Wallace 10th of Riccartonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1424年8月17日にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンムセルブルグで29歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドミドロスアンゴアブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Cathcart(이)가 1395년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 캐스카트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alan Cathcart(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Janet Maxwell(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1415년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 Sir John Wallace 10th of Riccarton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1424년 8월 17일 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 무셀버그에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 고레브리지에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Cathcart родилась в 1395 г. в местности каткарт, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Alan Cathcart было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Janet Maxwell, 18. Она умерла 17 августа 1424 г. в местности мусселбург, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 29 лет и была похоронена в местности горебридж, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания.
